rationale: The cited paper appears to be a plausible methodological predecessor rather than a generic mention: it develops an augmented Kalman filter for nonlinear state-space advertising dynamics, while the citing paper explicitly positions itself as extending Kalman-style parametric marketing dynamics with new Bayesian nonparametric state-space estimators. Given this clear method/domain overlap (dynamic advertising response modeling) and a confirmed DOI match, this pair is worth full relation judgment.
